index.title: index
index.content:
hi, i’m kaitwillows, and this is my website. i mostly use it as a dump for the things i create (mostly music), and whatever else i want to throw at it.
posts: blips | yips | yaps | songHarts
pages: me and my site | roadmap

pages/me-and-my-site.title: me and my site
pages/me-and-my-site.content:
hihi, i’m kaitlyn (she / he / they). i’m doing a cs/math degree, and i like to write music.
i do not use genAI in anything i create

outlinks are at the bottom of the site,

posts/2026-04-04-16-50.title: idk, catsitting, yap, music, nixos
posts/2026-04-04-16-50.content:
also i’m catsitting, and fucking around with nixos when i could maybe be studying for some of the classes that i might not fail. i’ve spent like, three days straight just configuring nixos help
(ill add a picture later)
i really want to start making music again. i feel like i just have to solve the problem of nixos in its entirety and then ill have a reproducable system for making music. genuinely the thing that pisses me off most about digital music creation is how absolutely volatile it is. ive tried portable reaper installs with plugins and samples supposedly saved in a single folder, and yet, the few projects i worked on on there just didn’t save any audio files anywhere in the directory.
i have a feeling nixos might solve this? if i can figure out the neccissary evil of switching to linux native audio editing software, then in theory i should be able to include a nixos configuration and a project folder?????? (i don’t really understand nixos yet.
ugh i genuinely dont know (i think paid plugins, if any, will fuck everything over so hard but idk), i think it’d be much more useful to actually start making music.
i know it goes against the principle(s) of zen or whatever, but i feel kinda bothered by the idea of not being able to open a 5 year old project file and just have it play. i think it’s my programmer brain or whatever. git was made to create records of all changes ever. anyone can clone your repo, see how you built your project, and you never have to worry about being distructive.
music has always felt messier, i know if i dont have an external tool to track changes, i’ll make duplicate clips, and duplicate tracks every time i want to tweak a sound, then delete the old one when i think im done with it. idk, UGH it’s a real frustration. i assume theres solutions with tools, maybe in the linux world or whatever, but maybe that could be a project if it doesnt yet exist.

posts/2026-04-13-16-26.title: sitting desk, take 1
posts/2026-04-13-16-26.content:
i’ve been entranced by the idea of a sitting desk, but i didn’t want to go out and actually get one just yet, so this is my alternative :sob:

ignore the absolute mess my basement is regardless i am sorry.
it’s genuinely kinda insane i find sitting crosslegged like this already more enjoyable than using my office chair. idk if it was just the desk height, that made my feet too far from the ground when i adjusted my chair to match.
now i need to get  a fucking split ergonomic keyboard with the thumb modifier keys, idk

i kinda wanna just keep track of what i do every day with this site. like, i almost got a little bit through the rust book, in addition to setting up a scrappy ass sitting desk. the website was being weird, it didnt really show code examples? and i got the fucking unicorn error page :( what do you even do in this situation. (i went and read the ebook version idfk)

there’s still a couple things i want to do before i actually publish this website i think. but for now i have to fucking go to work aaa

posts/2026-05-03-13-19.title: i dont like spotify :(
posts/2026-05-03-13-19.content:
hihi idk, i feel like spotify keeps getting shittier for the following reasons:

spotify player usually doesn’t play the ends of songs properly
they use (at least to some extent,) ai for their lofi playlists

this, presumably, lets them avoid paying the already disgustingly low streaming royalties to real artists.


non-togglable ai generated summaries of every song you listen to
idk probably something else

posts/2026-05-23-23-24.title: blip: keshi - get it (cover) 7
posts/2026-05-23-23-24.content:
im debating just not finishing this idk
the guitar just needs to be filled out and it’d probably be good enough. it needs so much polish but i’m almost feeling too lazy for just the guitar idfk
i think it’d be so cool to have some sort of pseudo passive income, almost like a big risk big reward type job? idk, either entrepreneur software development maybe where your revenue is day to day consistent regardless of what you do each day, but it’s only there because you did work at some point and it now exists to be sold. but i think it would be really cool to be able to do that with music; just have enough money to work on what you want full time, and eventually just make something you can release, and get your revenue like that. i dont want a lot, just enough to live and chill i guess. yap yuap yap yap yap
i kinda just wanna be unemployed forever (kinda not really idfk atp) let the world be all sunshine and rainbows
NO but like, the thing is, for me to even be able to buy a ticket in the one-in-five-trillion lottery of making any sort of money with music, i actually have to put in effort, and make shit sound good, but im too lazy idfjkasd nfjkahsdfilkajsdfkljas dklfj give me a couple weeks jnust wait
